issue.title: Promote WP_Http request nonblocking adapter to WPHX PHP IR

artifacts.role: WPHX PHP core IR/printer support for try/catch and PHP constants
WordPress-profile WP_Http::request nonblocking adapter plan emitted from structured IR
runner requiring request adapter template absence and IR feature evidence before accepting WPHX-312.65 generated shell
candidate manifest recording request nonblocking IR feature evidence
raw-block/template policy manifest with zero WordPress-profile adapter templates

claims: The bounded WP_Http::request nonblocking adapter is no longer emitted from a compiler-owned raw PHP adapter template.
The request public-boundary PHP shape is emitted from structured WPHX PHP core IR.
The WPHX-312.65 nonblocking oracle/candidate runner still passes and requires the request adapter template to be absent.
The WordPress adapter profile currently has zero PhpRawBlock occurrences and zero active adapter templates.
non_claims: This does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership, broad request orchestration ownership, or whole-file WP_Http ownership.
This does not claim live Requests network I/O, redirect following, transport execution, upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity, installed WordPress distribution behavior, or broader HTTP ownership.
This does not claim WPHX PHP is a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
